vitals.feed_zones: Semi-autonomous with base camp support at Arlon

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 73. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 69.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Base camp at Arlon provides dormitories, showers, and meals. Participants return to base camp after each loop on the 500km+ distances.[1]

final_verdict.one_liner: A distinctive European ultra-cycling challenge combining Benelux geography, cycling heritage, and demanding terrain for serious endurance cyclists.

videos.title: UCI - Gran Fondo World Series - Belgium
videos.description:
Probably the worst race I ever attended, not only due to the crash ...

It was a last minute decision, I bought someone's ticket as the original distance of 180km with 2500m of elevation had changed into 140km with 1450Hm, much more made for me.
After my great race at Gravel Turnhout I got sick 2 days later, viral infection and fever + also an issue with my left tigh (contraction ?), at the end of that week I couldn't ride a bike anymore nor get down the stairs. After 3 weeks it was becoming better so I was planning to use this race as an extra training session to get back in shape ...

But it didn't went as planned, a "huge" crash after only 8km made the race cancelled after 17km, there were no ambulances anymore to follow the race.
Crashing is part of racing, but this time the organisation is there to blame, all licensed riders had to start together, from age 19 to 59, men and women, all together. Almost 500 people which is asking for problems.

On the other hand the cancellation was also a bit of shame as I was riding in the leadgroup with only one other rider of my age group (50+), as the pace of that young dudes was way to high I decided to let myself drop on the first big climb, I also saw that the other one was dropped so my chances were still good and I had time to recover, I waited on group 2 which pace was much more ok for me untill we got stopped by marshalls and they told us the race was over, a shame but understandable !

KEYPOINTS :
01:05 already first chaos as the lead car didn't moved in time
11:45 major crash
17:45 detached from the lead group on the 1st climb
20:15 got catched by group 2
28:11 end of the race after 17km

videos.title: What does it take to WIN a GranFondo!? - Les Trois Ballons 2022
videos.description:
How hard is it to Win a Granfondo? Or even just stay with the first group? In this race I threw all my usual pacing strategy out the window and went FULL GAS uphill with the first group to see if I'd be able to keep up. 
At the time of this race my estimated FTP was about 385w at 82kg. I can push a lot of power but I'm a big guy so definitely in a disadvantage on the steeper climbs. This Granfondo is called Les Trois Ballons. It's in the Vosges in France, an area also featured in the Tour de France. The race is one of the bigger granfondo races in Europe by the same organisation of ''La Marmotte'', a race I did in 2017&2017. Les 3 Ballons goes over 6 cilmbs adding up to 4000m+ of elevation in 178km, so it's a big one. With a group of friends we made it into a cycling weekend with the guys.

key_challenges.name: Final climb
key_challenges.description: The last 15k contains the hardest climb with the final part being 24 percent gradient on gravel.

key_challenges.name: Crosswinds
key_challenges.description: Pretty damn windy conditions that create challenges and some carnage in the crosswinds during Belgian races.

terrain_notes.text: Technical circuits with a lot of variety, weaving through different little housing estates with a couple of little climbs.
The final climb is very hard with 24 percent gradient sections on gravel surface.

gear_mentions.text: Double chainring setup with 39 front and 34 in the back still allows shifting pretty light for climbing.
Hutchinson tires are described as very grippy.

race_day_tips.text: Don't throw pacing out the window - usually start conservative and settle in at your own pace rather than racing to win from the start.
Belgian riders are much more chilled before the start - everyone just rocks up like five minutes before, unlike UK racing.
Bring a car to races for a base, especially if some riders don't finish - gives you somewhere to get changed and store bags, phones and wallets.

additional_quotes.text: We turned up to the race today at an hour before we turned up at 2 p.m. I'm like one of the first people here it's quiet it's mandalite the difference between UK I'm Belgium racism but anyway
Bring a car to races for a base, especially if some riders don't finish - gives you somewhere to get changed and store bags, phones and wallets rather than stand around in kit all day

rider_intel.search_text: Belgian racing features technical circuits with challenging climbs and crosswind conditions. The Race Across Belgium includes courses weaving through housing estates with multiple climbs, including brutal final ascents with 24 percent gradients on gravel. Riders describe the terrain as technical with varied surfaces. Weather conditions include high humidity and strong crosswinds that create challenges and potential crashes. Belgian race culture is notably more relaxed than UK racing, with riders arriving just minutes before start times rather than hours early. Prize money extends down to 30th place with decent payouts. Courses typically feature multiple laps of 7-15km circuits with closed roads and strong community support. The racing is described as highly dynamic where situations can change rapidly, requiring strategic pacing rather than aggressive early moves.
